defs: A boat with an open well amidships. It is used in spearing fish.

records.ety: [OE. trowen, AS. trewan to trust, believe, fr. trew trust, trewe true, faithful. See True.]

defs: To believe; to trust; to think or suppose.
quotes: So that ye trow in Christ, and you baptize.
A better priest, I trow, there nowhere none is.
It never yet was worn, I trow.
What tempest, I trow, threw this whale . . . ashore?
notes: ☞ I trow, or trow alone, was formerly sometimes added to questions to express contemptuous or indignant surprise.
